title: Arctic sovereignty & sea-lanes
context: Climate change has opened the Northern Sea Route (NSR, Russian coast) and Northwest Passage (NWP, Canadian archipelago) for longer seasons. Continental-shelf claims (Lomonosov Ridge: Russia, Canada, Denmark) overlap over the North Pole and are unsettled at the UN CLCS. Russia has re-militarized its Arctic and requires 45-day notice for warships on the NSR; Canada regulates NWP transit. The US (not a UNCLOS party) insists both are international straits (freedom of navigation) and defined its own extended continental

parties.name: Canada
parties.endState: Restore Canada's place as an international Arctic leader.
parties.redLine: Not maintaining full control of the NWP as a Canadian trade route through Canadian internal waters.
parties.batna: Absent a deal, fall back to the alternate end-state: unilaterally defend and secure Canada and its Arctic residents, keep regulating the NWP as internal waters,

parties.name: China
parties.endState: Understand, protect, develop, and participate in the governance of the Arctic.
parties.redLine: No Russian (or other) restrictions on freedom of navigation and innocent passage for Chinese vessels along the NSR.
parties.batna: If denied full Arctic Council membership, seek enhanced responsibilities/roles as observer and play the long game to erode resistance; keep exploiting the legal

parties.name: Denmark
parties.endState: Greenland's territorial integrity is preserved and its resources developed per its people's desires, with the continental-shelf determination resolved.
parties.redLine: Any Chinese military presence on Greenland or in its surrounding waters.
parties.batna: Fall back from the primary goal to the alternate: a peaceful, international-law-based resolution of boundary disputes while preserving the status quo (Pituffik

parties.name: Finland
parties.endState: Strengthen the delicate balance between relations with Russia and inclusion in the EU and NATO.
parties.redLine: Any attempt to make China a member state of the Arctic Council.

parties.name: Norway
parties.endState: Norwegian territorial integrity is preserved, and its resources developed by its people.
parties.redLine: No agreement or regulation limiting Norway's EEZ surrounding Svalbard.
parties.batna: Maintain the status quo under the Svalbard Treaty and UNCLOS, keeping the Temporary Fisheries Protection Zone (TFPZ) in place unilaterally until the Svalbard EE

parties.name: Russia
parties.endState: Continued economic development of the Arctic (resource base expansion via hydrocarbon exploration/extraction; maintain favorable military posture).
parties.redLine: Not maintaining full control of the NSR as a Russian trade route.

parties.name: United States
parties.endState: Defend U.S. sovereignty in the Arctic and maintain a constant presence, including military, economic, and conservation interests.
parties.redLine: No Russian control/military oversight of the NSR.
